input.prompt:
I just upgraded my .NET MAUI app from .NET 9 to .NET 10. On iOS, my content
is now going behind the status bar and the Dynamic Island notch area. In .NET 9
I was using `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"` and it worked fine. What's the correct
way to handle this in .NET 10? Show me the XAML.

input.initialPrompt:
I just upgraded my .NET MAUI app from .NET 9 to .NET 10. On iOS, my content
is now going behind the status bar and the Dynamic Island notch area. In .NET 9
I was using `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"` and it worked fine. What's the correct
way to handle this in .NET 10? Show me the XAML.

data.content:
In .NET 10 MAUI, `ios:Page.UseSafeArea` was replaced with the **cross-platform** `SafeAreaRegions` property. Remove the iOS namespace and use:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             SafeAreaRegions="All"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage">

    <!-- your content -->

</ContentPage>
```

**Key differences:**

| .NET 9 (old) | .NET 10 (new) |
|---|---|
| `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"` | `SafeAreaRegions="All"` |
| iOS-only, required `xmlns:ios` | Cross-platform, no extra namespace |
| Boolean on/off | Granular: `None`, `All`, `Top`, `Bottom`, `Left`, `Right` |

You can also combine sides — e.g., `SafeAreaRegions="Top,Bottom"` to only inset vertically while letting content bleed to the edges horizontally.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI migration issue from .NET 9 to .NET 10 regarding safe area handling on iOS. This is a known breaking change in .NET 10 MAUI.

In .NET 10, the `ios:Page.UseSafeArea` attached property was removed/deprecated. The replacement is to use the new `SafeAreaRegions` property on `Page` (or other layout elements).

Let me provide the correct answer:

In .NET 10 MAUI, `ios:Page.UseSafeArea` was replaced with a cross-platform `Page.SafeAreaRegions` property. 

The new property offers more granular control over safe area insets. I'm setting up a ContentPage with the `SafeAreaRegions` attribute set to "All" to replicate the old behavior, and the enum supports individual values like `None`, `Top`, `Bottom`, `Left`, and `Right` for more precise control over which edges respect the safe area.
